title: Password hashes leaked to clients in account list responses
description: The receiveAccountListUsers function sends the password hash of every user account to any client with the 'wired.account.account.list_accounts' privilege. Similarly, receiveAccountReadUser (via accountUserMessage) sends the password hash at line 2478. Even though these are SHA-256 hashes, exposing them allows offline brute-force or rainbow table attacks. Any user with list_accounts/read_accounts privilege can extract all password hashes from the server.
recommendation: Never send password hashes to clients. Replace the password field with an empty string or a boolean 'has_password' flag. If password comparison is needed for admin operations, do it server-side only.
fix: Removed wired.account.password field from receiveAccountListUsers and accountUserMessage response builders
